strategic_profile.executive_summary: Tulane University is a highly selective, mid-sized private research institution located in New Orleans. It is renowned for its mandatory community service curriculum, its unified undergraduate college model that encourages interdisciplinary exploration, and a highly strategic admissions process that relies heavily on Early Decision.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Tulane's overall acceptance rate has plummeted to roughly 14%, but the university admits over 60% of its class through binding Early Decision rounds.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants treating Tulane as a target or reach school must apply Early Decision to have a statistically viable chance of admission.

analyst_takeaways.insight: All undergraduates are admitted to the Newcomb-Tulane College rather than specific majors.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students have the freedom to explore and double-major across business, public health, liberal arts, and STEM without restrictive internal transfer processes.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Tulane is deeply integrated into New Orleans and mandates service-learning courses for graduation.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants should emphasize civic engagement, volunteerism, and a specific desire to interact with the New Orleans community in their application materials.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Vibrant and highly active social scene; frequently ranked at the top for student happiness and quality of life. Strong 'work hard, play hard' culture.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Urban campus accessible via the St. Charles Avenue streetcar line, offering easy access to the broader cultural offerings of New Orleans while maintaining a distinct campus 'bubble'.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Carnegie R1 designated institution with extensive undergraduate research opportunities, notably in public health, tropical medicine, and biomedical sciences.

current_status.test_policy_details: Test-optional for the 2025-2026 admissions cycle. The university evaluates submitted scores using a superscore policy.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist behavior is highly unpredictable year-to-year based on yield.
Waitlist admit rate reported near 0% for this cycle.

gpa.notes: Average unweighted GPA is 3.70; 13% of enrolled students held a perfect 4.0 GPA.

testing.policy_note: 14% of admitted students submitted SAT; 31% submitted ACT.

colleges.name: Newcomb-Tulane College
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: All undergraduate applicants are admitted here regardless of intended major. Best for students who want academic flexibility and interdisciplinary studies.
colleges.housing_profile: Traditional residence halls with highly active social integration and living-learning communities.
colleges.student_archetype: Civically engaged, highly social, academically rigorous interdisciplinary thinkers.

majors.name: Finance
majors.special_requirements: Housed within the A.B. Freeman School of Business.
majors.notes: Most popular major at the university.

majors.name: Public Health
majors.special_requirements: Requires completion of a capstone experience. Housed in the Weatherhead School of Public Health and Tropical Medicine.
majors.notes: Unique 45-credit undergraduate program focused on social determinants of health and global health policy.

majors.name: Psychology
majors.notes: Highly popular major housed in the School of Science and Engineering.

supplemental_requirements.details: Optional but highly recommended 'Why Tulane?' statement.
Optional supplements/portfolios may be uploaded to the Green Wave Portal for scholarship consideration.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Commitment to community service and alignment with the unique culture of New Orleans are distinct differentiators.

major_selection_tactics: Major selection has negligible impact on admission odds since all applicants are admitted into the unified Newcomb-Tulane College.
college_ranking_tactics: Apply Early Decision I or II. Tulane employs heavy yield protection, filling the vast majority of its class through binding early applications.
Show profound demonstrated interest by attending virtual events, opening emails, and submitting the optional 'Why Tulane' essay.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Not applicable; students are not locked into their selected application major.

scholarships.name: Stamps Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Covers tuition, room, board, and fees, plus enrichment fund.

scholarships.name: FIRST Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Renewable award for students who participated in FIRST Robotics.

scholarships.name: Community Service Fellowship
scholarships.benefits: Provides cohort experience and leadership programming.

ap_policy.general_rule: Awards credit for scores of 4 or 5 on most AP exams.
ap_policy.usage: Can satisfy core requirements, except for specific restricted Tier-1 writing courses.

ib_policy.general_rule: Awards credit for scores of 5, 6, or 7 on most Higher Level exams.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Requires a grade of C or better. Maximum of 15 credits from dual enrollment during high school.

what_it_takes: Exceptional GPA and rigorous high school coursework.
Strong institutional fit articulated through essays.
common_activities: Leadership in student government or organizations
essay_tips: Articulate a specific, genuine reason for wanting to live and study in New Orleans.
Discuss a deep commitment to community engagement.
red_flags: Applying Regular Decision without exceptional academic credentials.
insights: Transferring is statistically much easier than applying as a freshman (60% vs 14% acceptance rate).
The 'Tulane Bubble' is real, but the university actively pushes students into the city through mandatory service-learning.
